commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

generation.compiledCode: import{jsx as r,jsxs as a}from"react/jsx-runtime";import{Card as m,Stack as i,Box as s,Divider as v,Heading as e,Text as o}from"@ggui-ai/design/primitives";function S(c){const{city:n,temperature:d,condition:g,humidity:l,windSpeed:u,unit:p="\xB0C",forecast:b}=c;return r(m,{shadow:"md",padding:24,radius:"lg",border:!0,"aria-label":`Weather in ${n}`,children:a(i,{gap:24,children:[a(i,{gap:4,children:[r(e,{level:3,children:n}),r(o,{variant:"bodySmall",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)",children:g})]}),a(i,{direction:"horizontal",justify:"between",align:"center",children:[a(e,{level:1,color:"var(--ggui-color-primary-600, #0284c7)",children:[d,p]}),a(i,{gap:4,children:[a(o,{variant:"caption",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)",children:["Humidity: ",l,"%"]}),a(o,{variant:"caption",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)",children:["Wind: ",u," km/h"]})]})]}),r(v,{}),r(i,{direction:"horizontal",justify:"between",gap:8,children:b.map((t,f)=>r(s,{padding:12,borderRadius:"var(--ggui-shape-radius-md, 8px)",background:"var(--ggui-color-surfaceVariant, #f4f4f5)",children:a(i,{align:"center",gap:8,children:[r(o,{variant:"overline",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)",children:t.day??"N/A"}),r(o,{size:"2xl","aria-label":`Condition: ${t.icon??"N/A"}`,children:t.icon??"\u2601\uFE0F"}),a(i,{direction:"horizontal",gap:8,children:[a(o,{variant:"bodySmall",weight:"bold",children:[t.high??0,"\xB0"]}),a(o,{variant:"bodySmall",color:"var(--ggui-color-onSurfaceVariant, #52525b)",children:[t.low??0,"\xB0"]})]})]})},f))})]})})}export{S as default};

issues.description: The forecast items lack hover states or visual feedback, making them feel static.
issues.fix: Add a simple hover effect (e.g., subtle scale or background opacity change) to the forecast Box elements.

issues.description: The layout of the current stats feels slightly unbalanced because the humidity/wind labels aren't visually grouped with icons or a clear container.
issues.fix: Use small icons (Droplet, Wind) alongside the humidity and wind labels to improve readability and visual hierarchy.

issues.description: Forecast items lack a descriptive role or specific aria-label regarding the forecast content, though the icons have aria-labels.
issues.fix: Add aria-label strings to the forecast cards to describe the forecast summary (e.g., 'Monday: Sunny, High 25, Low 15').

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows design token best practices, and cleanly maps props to the UI. It provides a solid foundation, needing only minor UX enhancements like hover states and improved data visualization for the stats section to be considered fully production-ready.

evaluation.critique: The component has good data handling and hierarchy, but has several design token violations including hardcoded fallback values with hex colors (e.g., '#52525b', '#0284c7'), hardcoded pixel values for padding (24, 12), and direct borderRadius styling instead of token references. The forecast strip lacks responsive behavior for smaller screens where horizontal scrolling may be needed.

commit.name: Multi-Step Survey Form
commit.description: Multi-step form with validation, progress tracking, and review
commit.prompt:
Build a multi-step survey form with 4 steps:
- Step 1: Text inputs for name (required) and email (required, must be valid email format) with inline validation
- Step 2: Radio button group for satisfaction rating (1-5 scale with labels from props)
- Step 3: Checkbox group for feature interests (options from props)
- Step 4: Textarea for open-ended comments (optional, with character counter, max 500 chars)

Requirements:
- Progress bar at the top showing current step (e.g., "Step 2 of 4")
- Back/Next navigation buttons (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Final review step showing all answers before submission
- Call props.onSubmit with all collected answers as a structured object
- All option labels and feature lists must come from props
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

featureOptions.description: Available feature options for the checkbox step

satisfactionLabels.description: Labels for satisfaction rating (e.g., Very Unsatisfied to Very Satisfied)

submit.label: Submit Survey
submit.description: Called with all collected answers as a structured object

commit.name: Kanban Task Board
commit.description: Column-based task board with task cards and state management
commit.prompt:
Build a Kanban-style task board with 3 columns: To Do, In Progress, Done.

Each column:
- Has a header with column name and task count
- Contains a vertical list of task cards
- Has an "Add Task" button at the bottom

Each task card displays:
- Task title (editable on double-click or via edit button)
- Priority badge (low/medium/high with color coding from design tokens)
- Assignee avatar or initials
- Due date (if set)
- A move button or dropdown to move the task to another column

Requirements:
- Tasks can be moved between columns via move controls (buttons or select dropdown)
- New tasks start in "To Do" column
- "Add Task" shows an inline form with title input and priority selector
- Track total tasks and completed count in a summary bar at the top
- All initial tasks come from props as an array with column assignment
- Call props.onTaskUpdate when a task is moved, edited, or created
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors, spacing, and shadows

tasks.description: Array of tasks with id, title, priority (low/medium/high), assignee initials, column ID, and optional dueDate

columns.description: Column definitions: [{id: "todo", name: "To Do"}, {id: "in-progress", name: "In Progress"}, {id: "done", name: "Done"}]

taskUpdate.label: Task Updated
taskUpdate.description: Called when a task is moved, edited, or created. Payload: { action, taskId, data }

issues.description: Column headers lack the requested task count indicator.
issues.fix: Append a badge or text indicating the count of tasks within that specific column in the Heading component.

issues.description: Tasks cannot be edited via double-click as requested, only through a button.
issues.fix: Add an onDoubleClick handler to the task title text element to trigger the editing state.

issues.description: The Move 'Select' component labels are not descriptive enough for screen readers when multiple exists on a page.
issues.fix: Use aria-label='Move task to different column' on the Select component to distinguish it from other form fields.

issues.description: Due date display is missing from the task cards.
issues.fix: Add a Text component rendering the task.dueDate within the task card layout.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional and well-structured, utilizing the design system primitives effectively. It misses a few specific feature requirements like the double-click trigger and the task count in headers, but the core logic for state management and column distribution is solid.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates solid layout and design token usage with proper spacing and semantic colors, but lacks visual hierarchy refinement (task count in column headers not shown) and interactive polish (missing hover states, transitions, and loading feedback). Data presentation is good overall, though the task count display in requirements is not visible in individual column headers.

violations: Hardcoded string "medium" found — should be a prop default

commit.name: Interactive Periodic Table
commit.description: CSS Grid periodic table with category coloring, search, and detail panel
commit.prompt:
Build an interactive periodic table of elements.

Layout:
- CSS Grid: 18 columns. Place each element using gridRow and gridColumn from its data.
- Each cell: symbol (large, centered), atomic number (small, top-left)
- Color-code by category using design tokens (e.g., noble-gas → one color, halogen → another)
- Search input at top — filters by name or symbol (case-insensitive)
- Click an element → detail panel below grid shows: name, symbol, atomicNumber, atomicWeight, category
- Legend showing each category with its color

All element data comes from props.elements. Use design system CSS variables.

issues.description: Missing atomic number position formatting (atomic number is centered in the card instead of top-left).
issues.fix: Use a Flex layout with justify-content: flex-start and align-items: flex-start on the Card internal container.

issues.description: The grid cells lack hover/active feedback states.
issues.fix: Add a pseudo-class or hover style to the grid cell wrapper to provide visual feedback (e.g., scale or border change) when interacting with elements.

issues.description: Keyboard navigation does not cycle through the 18-column grid effectively.
issues.fix: Implement arrow key navigation logic or ensure the tab order is intuitive and handles the grid structure properly.

issues.description: The legend is missing color indicators (only text labels exist).
issues.fix: Add a small circular swatch beside each category label in the legend to visually map back to the periodic table cells.

evaluations.critique: The component successfully implements the complex CSS grid layout and filtering requirements with clean code structure. It is highly functional but could benefit from improved visual feedback on interactions and a more robust legend for better UX clarity.

evaluation.critique: The component demonstrates strong design token usage and proper grid implementation for the periodic table layout. However, the detail panel could have better visual separation and the legend lacks a clear title; additionally, the search filtering could benefit from more prominent visual feedback when no results match.

commit.name: Chat Interface
commit.description: Chat UI with message list, input area, and send callback
commit.prompt:
Build a chat interface component. Layout:

Header:
- Chat title from props
- Participant count badge

Message list:
- Scrollable area showing all messages from props
- Each message shows: sender name, message text, formatted timestamp
- Messages from the current user (where sender matches props.currentUser) align right with primary color background
- Messages from others align left with surfaceVariant background
- Group consecutive messages from same sender

Input area at the bottom:
- Text input field for composing messages
- Send button (disabled when input is empty)
- Call props.onSend with { text, timestamp: new Date().toISOString() } on click or Enter

Requirements:
- All data from props — no hardcoded messages
- Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing
- Auto-scroll to bottom of message list on mount

currentUser.description: Current user name (for right-aligning own messages)

messages.description: Array of messages with sender name, text, and ISO timestamp

example.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm

sendMessage.label: Send Message
sendMessage.description: Called when user sends a message. Payload: { text, timestamp }

messages.text: Hey team, the design review is at 3pm
Got it, I will prepare the mockups
Also, can someone review the color palette?
I pushed the latest changes to the design branch
I will take a look after the meeting

issues.description: Requirement to group consecutive messages from the same sender was ignored.
issues.fix: Implement a logic loop or reduce function to cluster consecutive messages by the same sender into a single block.

issues.description: The component uses a custom 'window.sendMessage' hack instead of calling the requested 'onSend' prop.
issues.fix: Add an 'onSend' callback to the Props interface and invoke it within handleSendMessage.

issues.description: The component initializes state from props (props.messages) without syncing if the prop changes.
issues.fix: Use props.messages directly for rendering, or use useEffect to sync state if prop updates are intended to be persistent.

issues.description: The input lacks an associated label visually, and the error state/input interaction is basic.
issues.fix: Ensure the 'label' prop on the Input component is rendered or use a visually hidden label for screen readers.

evaluations.critique: The component is functional, responsive, and adheres well to design system constraints. It misses the specific requirement to group consecutive messages and relies on non-standard global window functions rather than the requested props contract, which hinders reusability.

evaluation.critique: The component has a solid overall structure with good flex layout and scrolling behavior, but uses hardcoded pixel values (paddingX={12}, paddingY={8}, borderRadius={12}) instead of design tokens, and the primary color (primary-600) is hardcoded rather than using semantic tokens. Message grouping by sender is not implemented despite being specified in requirements.

commit.name: Stock Ticker Dashboard
commit.description: Stock price display with color-coded changes and responsive grid
commit.prompt:
Build a stock ticker dashboard that displays stock prices. Layout:
- Header with "Market Watch" title and a "Last Updated" timestamp from props
- Responsive grid of stock cards (3 per row on desktop, 1 on mobile)
- Each card shows: ticker symbol (large, bold), company name, current price formatted with 2 decimals, change amount, and change percentage
- Color coding: green text/border for positive change, red for negative change
- A subtle arrow icon (▲ or ▼) next to the change percentage

All stock data comes from props. Use design system CSS variables for all colors and spacing.

stocks.description: Array of stocks with symbol, name, price, change, changePercent

lastUpdated.description: ISO timestamp of last price update

issues.description: The requirement requested a colored border for the cards based on performance, but only the text color is currently implemented.
issues.fix: Add a 'border' or 'stroke' prop to the Card component that uses the calculated trendColor when the variant is active.

issues.description: The stock cards are purely static and lack hover states or interactive cues.
issues.fix: Add a simple hover effect (e.g., scale slightly or increase shadow intensity) to provide better tactile feedback for dashboard items.

evaluations.critique: The component is clean, well-structured, and correctly handles the display requirements using design tokens. The use of CSS grid for the responsive layout is appropriate, and accessibility is handled well with aria-live updates.

evaluation.critique: The component has solid responsive grid layout and good data presentation, but relies on fallback hardcoded hex colors in multiple places (e.g., '#52525b', '#16a34a', '#dc2626') which undermines the design token system. The polish is decent with transitions and loading states, though missing hover/focus states on cards themselves.

commit.name: Onboarding Wizard
commit.description: Multi-step onboarding flow with step indicator and validation
commit.prompt:
Build a 3-step onboarding wizard. Steps:

Step 1 - Profile Setup:
- Name input (required)
- Email input (required, validate email format)
- Avatar selection (choose from 4 emoji avatars)

Step 2 - Preferences:
- Role selection (dropdown: Developer, Designer, Manager, Other)
- Notification toggle (email notifications on/off)
- Theme preference (light/dark toggle)

Step 3 - Review & Confirm:
- Summary of all selections from steps 1-2
- "Go Back" button to edit
- "Complete Setup" button to submit

Requirements:
- Step indicator at top showing current step (1/2/3) with progress bar
- Back/Next navigation (Next disabled until required fields valid)
- Smooth transition between steps
- Call props.onComplete with all collected data on final submit
- All initial values from props (for editing existing profile)
- Use design system CSS variables for all styling

initialProfile.description: Pre-filled profile data for editing (all fields optional)

avatarOptions.description: Available avatar emojis to choose from

roleOptions.description: Available role options for the dropdown

complete.label: Complete Setup
complete.description: Called with all profile data when user completes onboarding

issues.description: The progress bar animation/visual container logic is slightly misaligned; the inner bar height is small and hard to see.
issues.fix: Increase height of the progress bar track and bar for better visibility.

issues.description: The avatar selection buttons are not announced as part of a group, and the summary view lacks semantic list structure.
issues.fix: Wrap avatar selection in a fieldset/radiogroup and use a <ul>/<li> structure for the review summary.

issues.description: The 'Next' button does not have a 'disabled' state visual property while validation fails.
issues.fix: Add logic to set the 'disabled' prop on the 'Next' button based on current form state validation.

evaluations.critique: The component is well-structured, follows the design system accurately, and implements the logic effectively. The code is clean and modular. A few minor polish adjustments to accessibility and button states would elevate this to production-perfect.

evaluation.critique: The component has strong structure and mostly correct design token usage, but has hardcoded numeric values (padding: 16, minWidth: '48px') where design tokens should be used, and the review step displays raw camelCase keys instead of user-friendly labels. The progress bar animation references a non-existent 'ggui-fadeIn' keyframe.
